title: Chapter XV. 14, 15.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord Jesus had commended the love which He manifested toward us in dying for us, and had said, “Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends,” He added, “Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What great condescension! when one cannot even be a good servant unless he do his lord’s commandments; the very means, which only prove men to be good servants, He wished to be those whereby His friends should be known.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the condescension, as I have termed it, is this, that the Lord condescends to call those His friends whom He knows to be His servants.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, to let us know that it is the duty of servants to yield obedience to their master’s commands, He actually in another place reproaches those who are servants, by saying, “And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things that I say?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when ye say Lord, prove what you say by doing my commandments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not to the obedient servant that He is yet one day to say, “Well done, thou good servant; because thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy Lord”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One, therefore, who is a good servant, can be both servant and friend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us mark what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then are we to understand the good servant to be both servant and friend, when He says, “Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He introduces the name of friend in such a way as to withdraw that of servant; not as if to include both in the one term, but in order that the one should succeed to the place vacated by the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does it mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it this, that even in doing the Lord’s commandments we shall not be servants?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or this, that then we shall cease to be servants, when we have been good servants?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet who can contradict the Truth, when He says, “Henceforth I call you not servants?” and shows why He said so: “For the servant,” He adds, “knoweth not what his lord doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it that a good and tried servant is not likewise entrusted by his master with his secrets?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does He mean, then, by saying, “The servant knoweth not what his lord doeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be it that “he knoweth not what he doeth,” is he ignorant also of what he commands?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he were so, how can he serve?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or how is he a servant who does no service?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet the Lord speaks thus: “Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Henceforth I call you not servants.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Truly a marvellous statement!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Seeing we cannot serve the Lord but by doing His commandments, how is it that in doing so we shall cease to be servants?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I be not a servant in doing His commandments, and yet cannot be in His service unless I so do, then, in my very service, I am no longer a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us, brethren, let us understand, and may the Lord enable us to understand, and enable us also to do what we understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if we know this, we know of a truth what the Lord doeth; for it is only the Lord that so enables us, and by such means only do we attain to His friendship.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For just as there are two kinds of fear, which produce two classes of fearers; so there are two kinds of service, which produce two classes of servants.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a fear, which perfect love casteth out; and there is another fear, which is clean, and endureth for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fear that lies not in love, the apostle pointed to when he said, “For ye have not received the spirit of service again to fear.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he referred to the clean fear when he said, “Be not high-minded, but fear.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that fear which love casteth out, there has also to be cast out the service along with it: for both were joined together by the apostle, that is, the service and the fear, when he said, “For ye have not received the spirit of service again to fear.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it was the servant connected with this kind of service that the Lord also had in His eye when He said, “Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly not the servant characterized by the clean fear, to whom it is said, “Well done, thou good servant: enter thou into the joy of thy lord;” but the servant who is characterized by the fear which love casteth out, of whom He elsewhere saith, “The servant abideth not in the house for ever, but the Son abideth ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, He hath given us power to become the sons of God, let us not be servants, but sons: that, in some wonderful and indescribable but real way, we may as servants have the power not to be servants; servants, indeed, with that clean fear which distinguishes the servant that enters into the joy of his lord, but not servants with the fear that has to be cast out, and which marketh him that abideth not in the house for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us bear in mind that it is the Lord that enableth us to serve so as not to be servants.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this it is that is unknown to the servant, who knoweth not what his Lord doeth; and who, when he doeth any good thing, is lifted up as if he did it himself, and not his Lord; and so, glories not in the Lord, but in himself, thereby deceiving himself, because glorying, as if he had not received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us, beloved, in order that we may be the friends of the Lord, know what our Lord doeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is He who makes us not only men, but also righteous, and not we ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who but He is the doer, in leading us to such a knowledge?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “we have received not the spirit of this world, but the Spirit which is of God, that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever good there is, is freely given by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so because this also is good, by Him who graciously imparteth all good is this gift of knowing likewise bestowed; that, in respect of all good things whatever, he that glorieth may glory in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the words that follow, “But I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you,” are so profound, that we must by no means compress them within the limits of the present discourse, but leave them over till another.
